title: iQOO Z11x with 7,200 mAh battery and Dimensity 7400 SoC launched in India, price starts at ₹18,999
summary: iQOO has launched the Z11x 5G in India, featuring a 7200mAh battery, dual rear cameras, and a 6.76-inch Full HD+ display. Prices start at ₹18,999, offering models with up to 256GB storage, and the device supports military-grade durability and 4K video recording.

title: Adobe adds AI assistant to Photoshop, upgrades Firefly with new generative tools
summary: Adobe is launching a beta AI assistant for Photoshop on web and mobile, allowing paid users unlimited generations until April 9. The assistant simplifies editing tasks through voice or text commands, while Firefly Image Editor receives updates for improved image manipulation.

title: Google turns Chrome into an AI browser with Gemini support in India — top features, how to use and more
summary: Google is enhancing Chrome with Gemini AI integration. The feature is now available to users in India, Canada, and New Zealand. The AI can be used to summarize content, draft emails, and edit images.&nbsp;

title: Xiaomi 17, Xiaomi 17 Ultra with LEICA camera setup launched in India: Price, specs, launch offers and more
summary: Xiaomi launched the 17 series in India, featuring LTPO 120Hz displays, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processors, and LEICA camera setup. The Xiaomi 17 starts at ₹89,999, while the Ultra model begins at ₹1,39,999

title: Instagram down? Thousands report issues accessing social media platform, frustrated netizens say ‘whole thing is broken’
summary: Instagram is experiencing access issues, with over 10,000 user reports on Downdetector. The peak of complaints occurred around 8:59 AM India time.

title: Incentives, value addition boost made-in-India laptops
summary: Driven by government incentives, Made in India laptops now account for nearly 30% of domestic sales, up from less than 5% in 2024. Local manufacturers like Dixon and Syrma SGS are aggressively scaling capacity to offset a slowdown in the smartphone sector.

title: Michael Burry claims Nvidia used ‘mafia-like’ tactics to block AMD in Oracle data center deal, calls for antitrust probe
summary: Despite cancelling the planned 600-MW expansion at the Abilene site, Oracle and OpenAI’s broader plan to build 4.5 gigawatts of AI data centre capacity remains on track.

title: iPhone Fold leaks: Expected price, release date, specifications and more
summary: Apple's upcoming foldable iPhone, likely called iPhone Ultra, is likely to launch later this year. The phone could feature a dual rear camera setup and four cameras in total.&nbsp;

title: Russia-backed hackers target Signal and WhatsApp accounts: How the attack works, how to stay safe and more
summary: Dutch intelligence agencies warned of a global cyber campaign by Russian-backed hackers targeting Signal and WhatsApp accounts of officials and journalists. Hackers are using phishing techniques to gain access to the accounts, urging victims to provide SMS codes or PINs.

title: US-based Accel Leaders Fund may join Rapido’s $600 million round: Sources
summary: Along with the two investors, Accel’s India outfit is also participating in the transaction, which includes both primary and secondary components. Prosus and Accel India have also picked up stake in Rapido from food and grocery delivery firm Swiggy, which owned 12% of the bike taxi startup. Rapido’s

title: IAMAI urges Karnataka to defer gig worker welfare fee on ecomm firms till specifics are defined
summary: Per the Karnataka Platform-based Gig Workers (Social Security & Welfare) Act, 2025, ecommerce operators are required to pay 1% of their commission, subject to a cap, to fund gig workers’ welfare.

title: Eternal infuses Rs 450 crore into quick commerce business Blinkit
summary: This marks Eternal’s first capital infusion into Blinkit in 2026. Last year, the parent company pumped a total of Rs 2,600 crore into the quick commerce business.

title: Oracle prepares for layoffs, sets aside $2.1 billion for restructuring
summary: Oracle is preparing more job cuts as it increases reliance on artificial intelligence and funds expensive AI data centres, the Financial Times reported. The company has raised restructuring funds to $2.1 billion. Oracle earlier cut over 3,000 roles and may reduce thousands more as AI tools improve e

title: Amazon plans to invest $6.23 billion in Poland in 2026-2028
summary: Amazon plans to invest 23 billion zlotys ($6.23 billion) in Poland between 2026 and 2028, the company said in Warsaw. The investment follows 45 billion zlotys spent from 2012 to 2025. Amazon said the move reflects efforts to accelerate spending and expand its role in the Polish economy.

title: Infosys expands Mohali centre with Rs 290-crore investment, to add 3,000 seats
summary: Infosys has started expanding its Mohali Development Centre with a Rs 290 crore investment. The new facility will include a software development block and space for about 3,000 employees. The project aims to strengthen Infosys’ presence in North India and support AI-led solutions, cloud services and

title: Induction cooktops sold out on Amazon, quick commerce apps amid LPG cylinder shortage
summary: Induction cooktops are selling rapidly on ecommerce and quick commerce platforms after a shortage of commercial gas cylinders sparked panic buying. Products are now unavailable on Amazon, Zepto, Swiggy and Blinkit in major cities. The LPG supply pause has also forced restaurants and cloud kitchens t

title: Vibe coding startup Replit’s valuation triples to $9 billion in six months
summary: Vibe coding startup Replit raised $400 million, lifting its valuation to $9 billion, led by Georgian Partners with support from Shaquille O’Neal, Jared Leto, and others. The platform, used by 85% of the Fortune 500, helps users build software and AI apps, with annual recurring revenue soaring to $15

title: Razorpay taps Anthropic's Claude to build AI agents for payments
summary: Razorpay is building AI-powered payment agents using Anthropic’s Claude chatbot to handle tasks like recovering abandoned carts, retrying failed subscriptions, resolving disputes, and forecasting cash flow. The fintech also launched an AI-native platform for businesses to create custom agents, integ

title: India prepares smartphone export incentives in a boost for Apple
summary: India is preparing a new round of incentives for smartphone manufacturing that will link government subsidies to exports and greater use of locally made components, replacing the current Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme that ends on March 31.
